_comment: LLM-MAINTAINED MODULE MAP for @argo/desktop. Single source of truth for module boundaries; .dependency-cruiser.cjs turns it into lint rules mechanically — edit this map, never the generated rules. Refresh when the src/ structure changes. Managed by the setup-module-boundaries skill.

modules.name: shared
modules.comment: Cross-process contract: the Session state list and the union derived from it, the ship facts main observes, the event vocabulary, projected-state types, IPC channel names, the pure projection reducer, and the ribbon derivation (session facts → ship-flow render state). Types, frozen constants and pure functions only — no I/O, no framework. `SessionView` carries facts, never a rendered state, so grading stays on the renderer's side of the bridge. Imported by all three processes (renderer files reach it through the `@shared` alias), imports none of them.

modules.name: main
modules.comment: Electron main process. Its internals are private; the app is wired only from its own entry.

modules.name: preload
modules.comment: The context-bridge. Loaded by Electron via file path, not imported — nothing should import its internals.

modules.name: renderer
modules.comment: The UI (browser context). Talks to main only through the window bridge, never by import.

modules.name: renderer-shared
modules.comment: The renderer's shared leaf (feature-sliced `shared/`): the framework-primitive kit (`components/ui` + its icons) that every Pane composes, and the delivery-flow presentation vocabulary (`shared/delivery`: the Roster status word/icon and the `deliveryState` composition over the `@shared` ribbon derivation). A building block imported by two or more domains lives here, so every domain may import it and it imports no domain in return. External code reaches it only through the three barrels; its internals are private.

modules.name: roster
modules.comment: The Roster Pane (CONTEXT.md region): the session roster column and its rows. A feature slice — owns its `components/` and, as they arrive, its hooks/store/types. Composed by the screen through its barrel; its internals are private and it never reaches into another domain.

modules.name: activity
modules.comment: The Activity Pane (CONTEXT.md region; supersedes ADR-0009's `story/`): the Background Tasks roster — Runs, Agents, phases. A feature slice composed by the screen through its barrel; its internals are private and it never reaches into another domain.

modules.name: delivery
modules.comment: The Delivery Panel (CONTEXT.md region; supersedes ADR-0009's `work/`): the Delivery lifecycle and node drawers — DeliveryLifecycle + NodeDrawer, the CI card molecules, the LifecycleNode state→icon/tone vocabulary. Pure Views over the pre-derived `LifecycleModel` from `@shared`. A feature slice composed by the screen through its barrel; its internals are private and it never reaches into another domain.

modules.name: console
modules.comment: The Console Pane (CONTEXT.md region, cockpit-matrix.md R13): the ONE monospace surface, its channel strip and channel body, plus the channel vocabulary and the three derivations over it (`captureLabel`, `resolveActiveChannel`, `feedLines`). A feature slice composed by the screen through its barrel; its internals are private and it never reaches into another domain.

layers._comment: Two isolations. (1) Electron process isolation: the three processes must never import each other — the only channels are the preload bridge (renderer↔main) and Electron's own preload wiring (main→preload by path). All three may import `shared` (the cross-process type/pure-function contract); `shared` imports none of them. (2) Renderer feature-slice isolation: a domain (Pane) must not reach into another domain's internals (domains ⊥ domains); every domain may import `renderer-shared` (domains → shared); `renderer-shared` is a leaf that imports no domain (shared ⊥ domains).
